channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Waymo Has Stopped Testing Its Robotaxis in New York City and No One Knows What Happens Next
item.description: New York City's Mayor, Zohran Mamdani, was non-committal when it comes to the future.

item.title: The Best Anti-Meta Smart Glasses Are About to Have Tough Competition
item.description: If camera-equipped smart glasses aren't your thing, you're about to have a new pair at your disposal.

item.title: Gas prices aren’t the only factor fueling used EV sales
item.description: There are a growing number of reasons why used EVs have gained in popularity.

item.title: 'Like many small fires': Surge in ultra-hot data centers is creating extreme heat islands that may dramatically alter global climate
item.description: Hyperscale AI data centers produce significant local heat, affecting over 340 million people and creating immediate environmental challenges globally.

item.title: The Artemis 2 Mission Is Leaning in on the ‘Project Hail Mary’ Love
item.description: The crew of the ongoing space mission not only saw and loved the movie, they're referencing it in real time.

item.title: How to watch UConn vs Michigan: Free Streams, TV Channels for 2026 March Madness Final
item.description: All the ways to watch UConn vs Michigan live streams online – including for FREE – for the 2026 March Madness final at Lucas Oil Stadium.

item.title: Here’s How Many Americans Have Ever Thought About Shooting Someone Else
item.description: Most people who have ever seriously considered gun violence never tell anyone else about it, a recent study reveals.

item.title: As Meta Flounders, It Reportedly Plans to Open Source Its New AI Models
item.description: At least if it sucks, everyone will be able to see why.

item.title: Visible’s Pro+ plan is 50% off right now — and it even worked during Verizon’s outage
item.description: Visible’s top-tier Pro+ plan is now 50% off, dropping to under $20 a month—and after months of testing, it’s held up even during outages.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts break a record, name a crater
item.description: A few minutes before 2PM ET on Monday, the crew of Artemis II broke a record set 56 years ago by the ill-fated Apollo 13 mission - at over 248,655 miles, they have now traveled farther from Earth than any humans before them. They marked the occasion with a crater naming ceremony that left the […]

item.title: Can a picky eater find happiness with an adventurous foodie? Modern daters debate the gravity of relationship gaps
item.description: What happens when we become preoccupied with finding niche compatibility tests and categorizing the differences between partners? As if modern dating weren’t difficult enough, the internet has become obsessed with finding niche compatibility tests and categorizing the differences between partners, with a string of so-called relationship gaps going viral on platforms such as TikTok recently....

item.title: A Single Strike Won’t Shut Off the Gulf’s Desalination System
item.description: The Gulf’s water system is built with layers of backup, but it relies on continuous operation to hold.

item.title: 'The inference inflection has arrived': Nvidia pumps $2 billion into chipmaker Marvell to boost its AI factories to the next level — so does this mean it'll be working with Amazon Trainium soon?
item.description: Nvidia invests $2 billion in Marvell, integrating XPUs, photonics, and networking into NVLink Fusion to expand AI infrastructure globally

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S27 Ultra could get a new variant with major missing feature
item.description: Rumor has it that Samsung is going to introduce a fourth Galaxy S27 variant for next year's flagship launch.

item.title: FBI warns against foreign mobile apps like CapCut in new PSA
item.description: The FBI is warning Americans about the dangers of foreign-based mobile apps, specifically those from China.

item.title: All Hell Breaks Loose in a Tease of the End of ‘Daredevil: Born Again’ Season 2
item.description: So much happens in this teaser that Jessica Jones and Punisher showing up aren't even the biggest things.

item.title: Let Justin Timberlake and Tiger Woods be a warning: The body cam footage industry could come for any of us
item.description: Body cam of two different celebrity arrests have spawned memes but little reflection. We’re all one bad day away from becoming content against our will. One need not be a sadist to enjoy the deeply unflattering body cam footage of Tiger Woods’ recent drunk driving arrest. Even before factoring in anyone’s personal feelings about the peerlessly accomplished but past-his-prime athlete, or their feelings about drunk drivers in general, the photos are internet-gold that lend themselves easily to memes and jokes....
